eyebrow: If I Awaken in Los Angeles · Space 07 of 07 · Egress
title: The Real LA
summary: The audience walks back through the corridor where the show began — but Hollywood Boulevard is gone. The 110-ft LED wall now shows the audience itself, composed into a living mural of LA that breathes and reorganizes as people move through. The show's final argument made visual: the city was never the sign on the hill. It was the people in this room.
callout:
The audience exits Space 6 after the bow and re-enters the same corridor they walked through 100 minutes ago. Same dimensions, same LED wall, same Walk of Fame stars on the floor. But the content has been completely overwritten. The Hollywood Sign is gone. The Walk of Fame celebrities are gone. The dancers-as-tourists are gone. The wall now shows the audience itself — every face captured during the show, composed into an enormous, slowly-shifting mural of Los Angeles. The audience enters at the north end, walks the length of the wall, and exits through a doorway in the middle of the east wall into the lobby and gift shop.

The mural never settles. Every 30 seconds it reorganizes — a skyline made of faces, then a beach, then a freeway at dusk, then a neighborhood block, then a mountain range — always built from the same audience portraits, always rearranging. Three words hold underneath, slowly fading in and out: This is Los Angeles. The audience walks through, looks for themselves, photographs the wall, photographs themselves in front of it. The corridor is otherwise empty. No performers. No Tour Guide. No music — just the residual hum of the band's last chord from Space 6, slowly fading. The city does the talking.

legend.label: LED Mosaic Wall
legend.note: Same wall as Space 1 · 110 × 14 ft · audience faces compose LA

legend.label: Walk of Fame stars
legend.note: Now blank · existing brass star plates retained

legend.label: Viewing zone
legend.note: Full length of wall · audience lingers and photographs

legend.label: Flow lane + exit
legend.note: Mid-east-wall doorway to lobby · audience curves out at mid-corridor

beats.title: Re-entry · the room remembered
beats.body: The first audience members round the corner from Space 6 and re-enter the corridor. They recognize the room. Same proportions, same Walk of Fame stars on the floor, same massive west wall. For a half-second they expect the Hollywood content — the Sign, the cycling landmarks, the opening they walked in to. Instead, the wall is something else entirely. Recognition flickers into surprise. They slow down without realizing they've slowed down.

beats.title: The skyline forms
beats.body: The wall holds its first composition: the LA skyline, downtown towers and hills, rendered entirely from audience portraits arranged like mosaic tiles. From across the corridor it reads as a recognizable cityscape; up close, individual faces resolve. The text underneath, in slow Playfair italic: this is los angeles. It holds. The audience starts walking the length of the wall, scanning. Where am I?

tags.label: Last chord still fading · ambient hush

beats.title: The mural breathes
beats.body: Every 30 seconds the mosaic reorganizes. The skyline dissolves; the same audience faces drift into a new composition — a beach with surfers, then a freeway at dusk with brake-light streams, then a residential neighborhood with palm trees and bungalows, then a mountain ridgeline. Each composition holds for 30 seconds before the next morph. The audience never sees the same image twice in a single egress window. The text underneath cycles too — sometimes this is los angeles, sometimes just los angeles, sometimes blank.

beats.title: Finding yourself · photographing the wall
beats.body: The audience naturally arranges itself in two streams. Some walk the full length of the wall scanning for their face — they stop, point, laugh, photograph the wall, photograph themselves in front of it, show their friends. Others move along the east side of the corridor, taking it in but heading for the exit. The wall is photographable from every angle: the macro mural, the micro tile, the selfie. People linger. The corridor is full of soft phone-screen glow and quiet conversation. The Walk of Fame stars on the floor are dim, brass outlines only — they no longer demand attention.

tags.label: No performers · the audience is the show

beats.title: Exit · turning out into the lobby
beats.body: The audience reaches the middle of the corridor and turns right through the doorway in the east wall — into the lobby and gift shop. The exit is mid-wall, not at the end, so the audience naturally walks past half the mural before turning out, then sees the south half of the mural as they pivot toward the doorway. Some keep walking south first to take in the full length of the wall before doubling back to the exit. The wall does not fade — it keeps cycling for the next person, and the next. The band's last chord has finally dissolved entirely; the room is silent except for the soft murmur of the audience itself. The lobby is calm and warmly lit. Front doors open onto the street. The audience steps out of the warehouse and back into the actual city of LA — the one the show has been telling them about. The closing image of the show is not on a screen. It is the city outside the door.

tags.label: Band's chord finally gone · ambient room tone
Mid-corridor turn · east wall doorway to lobby

locked: Same physical corridor as Space 1 — no new build, content overwrite only
LED wall is the centerpiece · existing 110 ft × 14 ft LED wall reused
Display direction: Living Mosaic — audience portraits compose images of LA that reorganize every 30 seconds
Compositions cycle through multiple LA images: skyline, beach, freeway, neighborhood, mountains (final list TBD)
Subtitle text holds and fades: "This is Los Angeles."
No performers in the corridor on egress — audience experiences it alone
Tour Guide is not present (removes performance frame; audience owns the moment)
Walk of Fame stars on floor remain physical but dimmed — brass outlines only, no celebrity names lit
Sound: band's last chord from Space 6 finale slowly fading, then silence
Audience moves at their own pace — viewers stop near wall, pass-through lane on east side
Total dwell ~3–8 min per person; total egress window ~10–12 min
Transition to lobby/gift shop/street is direct and unmarked — no curtain call, no "exit through gift shop" framing
stillOpen: How audience photos are captured — to be designed separately as a tech workstream (red carpet, in-show capture, etc.)
Final list of LA compositions the mural cycles through — needs creative direction (skyline + how many others?)
Composition transition style — hard cut, dissolve, slow drift of tiles into new positions, or hybrid
Subtitle text variants — "this is los angeles" only, or rotating phrases (e.g., neighborhood names, show lines, blank intervals)
Whether the Tour Guide makes a silent presence at the corridor exit (handshake, nod) or is fully absent
Ambient sound — sustained band chord, ambient room tone, or full silence?
Walk of Fame star treatment — fully dim, faintly uplit, or replaced with audience names rotating?
Lobby/gift shop content — does it echo the mural (printed mural, photo download station) or stay neutral?
Photo delivery to audience — QR code, email, web gallery (tech workstream)
Consent / opt-out flow for audience photos (legal + production workstream)
What happens for audience members who don't appear in the mosaic (opted out, missed by capture) — placeholder tiles? Skip and move on?
Show-night reset — does the wall reset at end of night, or do photos accumulate across multiple shows?
